items.blurb: Jamahl Mosley Following Magic Win To Take 3-1 Series Lead: 'Right Now It Means Nothing' — The Orlando Magic defeated the Detroit Pistons 94-88 in Game 4 on Monday night to take a 3-1 series lead. "We have the advantage and now we have to try and make sure we keep that advantage."

items.title: Thunder Sweep Suns With 131-122 Game 4 Win
items.blurb: Thunder Sweep Suns With 131-122 Game 4 Win — The Oklahoma City Thunder swept the Phoenix Suns following their 131-122 win on Monday night. Shai Gilgeous-Alexander lead the Thunder with 31 points on 10-of-17 shooting from the field. Chet Holmgren added 24 points, with Ajay Mitchell added 22 points. The Thunder also shot 17-of-34 from the three-point line. Devin Booker lead the Suns with 24 points,

items.blurb: Oklahoma City Thunder sweep their way to second round — Facing elimination, the Phoenix Suns didn't lie down against the Oklahoma City Thunder. It wasn't enough to avoid a four-game sweep.

items.title: James Harden Notched Historic Career Feat On Monday
items.blurb: James Harden Notched Historic Career Feat On Monday —   Los Angeles Clippers guard James Harden entered Monday’s matchup against the Philadelphia 76ers needing just one point to reach a significant career milestone. The Clippers fell to Philadelphia in a closely contested game at the Wells Fargo Center on Tuesday, but Harden delivered 28 points along with six rebounds and five assists.

items.title: Franz Wagner To Undergo MRI On Sore Right Calf
items.blurb: Franz Wagner To Undergo MRI On Sore Right Calf — Orlando Magic forward Franz Wagner will undergo an MRI on his sore right calf on Tuesday. Wagner left the Magic's Game 4 win over the Pistons on Monday in the third quarter with the injury. Wagner added that he hopes to play in Game 5. With the Magic up 3-1 in the series, Game 5 will take place on Wednesday at Detroit.

items.title: Jayson Tatum: Double-doubles in defeat
items.blurb: Jayson Tatum: Double-doubles in defeat — Tatum tallied 24 points (8-19 FG, 4-12 3Pt, 4-7 FT), 16 rebounds, four assists and three steals in 41 minutes during Tuesday's 113-97 loss to the 76ers in Game 5 of the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs.
